description: Test prompts for evaluating the TF2.15 + MUSA + XLA custom-call workflow

evals.prompt: Use agentic evolution to reduce TF2.15 whole-model inference latency on MUSA. The optimized operator must be integrated into XLA through a custom call and validated in the user-provided target container.
evals.expected_output: Run baseline, collect hotspots, choose one target, wire it through LayerNorm-style XLA custom-call integration, and accept only if whole-model latency improves in the user-provided environment.

evals.prompt: A hotspot operator already has a faster local implementation, but the model did not speed up. Use this skill to diagnose the XLA custom-call path and explain what still needs to be integrated.
evals.expected_output: Check rewriter, custom-call bridge, runtime registration, and whole-model benchmark path before claiming success.

evals.prompt: Set up the first optimization iteration for a new hotspot using the LayerNorm example as the template and record the attempt in lineage.jsonl.
evals.expected_output: Prepare the baseline, initialize knowledge files, run XLA custom-call checks, and append a lineage record with accept/reject status.
